messages.content: You are the planning module of a patent-drafting agent. Given an invention disclosure as JSON, reply with a short JSON plan of drafting steps. Reply with JSON only.

{"id":"uspto-19206781","title":"METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR CLASSIFYING CONTENT OF WEB PAGES USING MACHINE LEARNING TECHNIQUES","inventors":["Evyatar SEGAL","Yehuda Alexander KRUPKA"],"technology_class":"G06F","summary":"A system and method for optimizing web content is presented. The method includes receiving contents of a web page, wherein the contents include at least a plurality of elements, each element including a markup language code; detecting at least one relevant element from the contents based on a predefined list of element types; extracting attributes from each element into an element feature vector; classifying each element based on the element feature vector of extracted attributes; and identifying at least one candidate element for web content optimization based on an element classification and the element feature vector.
Page 1 of 1



 
 ABST

 19206781

 1

 ABSTRACT

 A system and method for optimizing web content is presented. The method includes receiving contents of a web page, wherein the contents include at least a plurality of elements, each element including a markup language code; detecting at least one relevant element from the contents based on a predefined list of element types; extracting attributes from each element into an element feature vector; classifying each element based on the element feature vector of extracted attributes; and identifying at least one candidate element for web content optimization based on an element classification and the element feature vector.","details":"This listing of claims will replace all prior versions and listings of claims in the application:

 1

 1. (Currently amended) A method for optimizing web content, comprising:

 receiving contents of a web page, wherein the contents include at least a plurality of elements, each element including a markup language code;

 detecting, using a semantic similarity technique, at least one relevant element of the plurality of elements from the contents based on a semantic similarity to an element in a predefined list of element types;

 generating extracting attributes from each element into an at least one element feature vector representing the at least one relevant element in a vector database, wherein each feature of the least one element feature vector represents an attribute of the at least one relevant element;

 classifying, using a machine learning model trained on labeled examples of element feature vectors, each element based on the at least one element feature vector of extracted attributes; [[and]]

 identifying at least one candidate element of the at least one relevant element for web content optimization based on an element the classification for the at least one candidate element and the element feature vector representing the at least one candidate element;[[.]] and

 generating markup language code for at least one web-optimized element, wherein generating the markup language code for the at least one web-optimized element replaces the at least one candidate element to change the contents of the web page.

 Currently amended

 2

 2. (Previously presented) The method of claim 1
, further comprising:

 modifying the markup language code of the at least one candidate element.

 Previously presented

 3

 3. (Currently amended) The method of claim 1
, wherein identifying the at least one candidate element of the at least one relevant element for web content optimization further comprises:

 detecting when a value of a feature of the element feature vector representing the at least one candidate element is above a predetermined threshold distance from a target value; and

 when the value of the feature of the element feature vector is above the predetermined threshold distance, modifying the markup language code of the at least one candidate element to set the value of the feature of the element feature vector below the predetermined threshold distance.

 Currently amended

 4

 4. (Currently amended) The method of claim 1
, wherein extracting attributes from each element into the element feature vector further comprises further comprising:

 detecting, in the at least one relevant element, at least one of: positional attributes, textual attributes, structural attributes, visual attributes, and behavioral attributes.

 Currently amended

 5

 5. (Currently amended) The method of claim 1
, further comprising:

 detecting, using a semantic similarity technique, at least one collection of elements of the plurality of elements from the contents of a web page;

 generating extracting attributes from each collection into a at least one collection feature vector representing the at least one relevant collection in a vector database, wherein each feature of the least one collection feature vector represents an attribute of the at least one collection; 

 classifying, using a machine learning model trained on labeled examples of collection feature vectors, each collection based on the at least one collection feature vector of extracted attributes; [[and]]

 identifying at least one candidate collection of the at least one collection for web content optimization based on a collection the classification for the at least one candidate collection and the collection feature vector representing the at least one candidate collection[[.]]; and

 generating markup language code for at least one web-optimized collection, wherein generating the markup language code for the at least one web-optimized collection replaces the at least one candidate collection to change the contents of the web page.

 Currently amended

 6

 6. (Currently amended) The method of claim 5
, wherein identifying the at least one candidate collection of the at least one collection for web content optimization further comprises:

 detecting when a value of a feature of the collection feature vector representing the at least one candidate collection is above a predetermined threshold distance from a target value; and

 when the value of the feature of the collection feature vector is above the predetermined threshold distance, modifying the markup language code of the at least one candidate collection to set the value of the feature of the collection feature vector below the predetermined threshold distance.

 Currently amended

 7

 7. (Currently amended) The method of claim 5
, wherein extracting attributes from each collection into the collection feature vector further comprisesfurther comprising:

 detecting, in the at least one collection, at least one least one of: logical order, semantic consistency, density, visual hierarchy, functional grouping, and network traffic behavior.

 Currently amended

 8

 8. (Currently amended) The method of claim 1
, further comprising:

 generating a prompt for a large language model (LLM) of a generative AI system, the prompt generated based on at least a predetermined template and the markup language code of the at least one candidate element; and

 processing the generated prompt utilizing the LLM to generate a modified the markup language code of the at least one candidate web-optimized element.

 Currently amended

 9

 9. (Previously presented) The method of claim 1
, wherein the contents of a web page include a Document Object Model (DOM) of the web page.

 Previously presented

 10

 10. (Currently amended) The method of claim 5
, further comprising:

 receiving a training dataset including a plurality of pairs of feature vectors representing at least one element and corresponding ground-truth class labels;

 inputting the feature vectors and corresponding ground-truth class labels into the[[a]] machine learning model; and

 training the machine learning model using the training dataset to minimize a loss function that quantifies a difference between predicted class labels and the corresponding ground-truth class labels.

 Currently amended

 11

 11. (Previously presented) The method of claim 10
, further comprising:

 generating the training dataset using a generative AI system configured to execute an LLM.

 Previously presented

 12

 12. (Previously presented) The method of claim 5
, further comprising:

 receiving a dataset comprising a plurality of unlabeled feature vectors, each feature vector representing at least one element;

 inputting the feature vectors into an unsupervised machine learning model; and

 training the unsupervised machine learning model on the dataset to detect latent structures or groupings among the feature vectors based on a measure of feature similarity among the feature vectors.

 Previously presented

 13

 13. (Currently amended) A non-transitory computer-readable medium storing a set of instructions for optimizing web content, the set of instructions comprising:

 one or more instructions that, when executed by one or more processors of a device, cause the device to:

 receive contents of a web page, wherein the contents include at least a plurality of elements, each element including a markup language code;

 detect, using a semantic similarity technique, at least one relevant element from the contents based on a semantic similarity to an element in a predefined list of element types;

 generate extract attributes from each element into an at least one element feature vector in a vector database, wherein each feature of the at least one element feature vector represents an attribute of the at least one relevant element;

 classify, using a machine learning model trained on labeled examples of element feature vectors, each element based on the at least one element feature vector of extracted attributes; [[and]] 

 identify at least one candidate element of the at least one relevant element for web content optimization based on an element the classification for the at least one candidate element and the element feature vector representing the at least one candidate element;[[.]] and

 generate markup language code for at least one web-optimized element, wherein generating the markup language code for the at least one web-optimized element replaces the at least one candidate element to change the contents of the web page.

 Currently amended

 14

 14. (Currently amended) A system for optimizing web content comprising:

 a processing circuitry;

 a memory, the memory containing instructions that, when executed by the processing circuitry, configure the system to:

 receive contents of a web page, wherein the contents include at least a plurality of elements, each element including a markup language code;

 detect, using a semantic similarity technique, at least one relevant element from the contents based on a semantic similarity to an element in a predefined list of element types;

 generate extract attributes from each element into an at least one element feature vector in a vector database, wherein each feature of the at least one element feature vector represents an attribute of the at least one relevant element;

 classify, using a machine learning model trained on labeled examples of element feature vectors, each element based on the at least one element feature vector of extracted attributes; [[and]] 

 identify at least one candidate element of the at least one relevant element for web content optimization based on an element the classification for the at least one candidate element and the element feature vector representing the at least one candidate element;[[.]] and

 generate markup language code for at least one web-optimized element, wherein generating the markup language code for the at least one web-optimized element replaces the at least one candidate element to change the contents of the web page.

 Currently amended

 15

 15. (Previously presented) The system of claim 14
, wherein the memory contains further instructions which when executed by the processing circuitry further configure the system to:

 modify the markup language code of the at least one candidate element.

 Previously presented

 16

 16. (Currently amended) The system of claim 14
, wherein the memory contains further instructions that, when executed by the processing circuitry for identifying the at least one candidate element of the at least one relevant element for web content optimization, further configure the system to:

 detect when a value of a feature of the element feature vector representing the at least one candidate element is above a predetermined threshold distance from a target value; and 

 when the value of the feature of the element feature vector is above the predetermined threshold distance, modify the markup language code of the at least one candidate element to set the value of the feature of the element feature vector below the predetermined threshold distance.

 Currently amended

 17

 17. (Currently amended) The system of claim 14
, wherein the memory contains further instructions that, when executed by the processing circuitry for extracting attributes from each element into the element feature vector, further configure the system to:

 detect, in the at least one relevant element, at least one of:

 positional attributes, textual attributes, structural attributes, visual attributes, and behavioral attributes.

 Currently amended

 18

 18. (Currently amended) The system of claim 14
, wherein the memory contains further instructions which when executed by the processing circuitry further configure the system to:

 detect, using a semantic similarity technique, at least one collection of elements of the plurality of elements from the contents of a web page;

 generate extract attributes from each collection into a at least one collection feature vector in a vector database, whereineach feature of the least one collection feature vector represents an attribute of the at least one collection;

 classify, using a machine learning model trained on labeled examples of collection feature vectors, each collection based on the at least one collection feature vector of extracted attributes; [[and]] 

 identify at least one candidate collection of the at least one collection for web content optimization based on a collection the classification for the at least one candidate collection and the collection feature vector;[[.]] and

 generate markup language code for at least one web-optimized collection, wherein generating the markup language code for the at least one web-optimized collection replaces the at least one candidate collection to change the contents of the web page.

 Currently amended

 19

 19. (Currently amended) The system of claim 18
, wherein the memory contains further instructions that, when executed by the processing circuitry for identifying the at least one candidate collection of the at least one collection for web content optimization, further configure the system to:

 detect when a value of a feature of the collection feature vector representing the at least one candidate collection is above a predetermined threshold distance from a target value; and 

 when the value of the feature of the collection feature vector is above the predetermined threshold distance, modify the markup language code of the at least one candidate collection to set the value of the feature of the collection feature vector below the predetermined threshold distance.

 Currently amended

 20

 20. (Currently amended) The system of claim 18
, wherein the memory contains further instructions that, when executed by the processing circuitry for extracting attributes from each collection into the collection feature vector, further configure the system to:

 detect, in the at least one collection, at least one least one of:

 logical order, semantic consistency, density, visual hierarchy, functional group, and network traffic behavior.

 Currently amended

 21

 21. (Currently amended) The system of claim 18
, wherein the memory contains further instructions which when executed by the processing circuitry further configure the system to:

 receive a training dataset including a plurality of pairs of feature vectors representing at least one element and corresponding ground-truth class labels;

 input the feature vectors and corresponding ground-truth class labels into [[a]]the machine learning model; and 

 train the machine learning model using the training dataset to minimize a loss function that quantifies a difference between predicted class labels and the corresponding ground-truth class labels.

 Currently amended

 22

 22. (Previously presented) The system of claim 21
, wherein the memory contains further instructions which when executed by the processing circuitry further configure the system to:

 generate the training dataset using a generative AI system configured to execute an LLM.

 Previously presented

 23

 23. (Previously presented) The system of claim 18
, wherein the memory contains further instructions which when executed by the processing circuitry further configure the system to:

 receive a dataset comprising a plurality of unlabeled feature vectors, each feature vector representing at least one element;

 input the feature vectors into an unsupervised machine learning model; and 

 train the unsupervised machine learning model on the dataset to detect latent structures or groupings among the feature vectors based on a measure of feature similarity among the feature vectors.

 Previously presented

 24

 24. (Previously presented) The system of claim 14
, wherein the memory contains further instructions which when executed by the processing circuitry further configure the system to:

 generate a prompt for a large language model (LLM) of a generative AI system, the prompt generated based on at least a predetermined template and the markup language code of the at least one candidate element; and 

 process the generated prompt utilizing the LLM to generate a modified the markup language code of the at least one candidate web-optimized element.

 Previously presented

 25

 25. (Previously presented) The system of claim 14
, wherein the contents of a web page include a Document Object Model (DOM) of the web page.

 Previously presented"}

transcript.reply:
{
  "drafting_plan": [
    "Extract core technical components from claims 1, 5, and 14 as independent invention concepts: element feature vector extraction, classification, candidate identification, and optimization via markup modification.",
    "Map each claim amendment to a corresponding independent claim: claim 1 → independent method claim; claim 5 → independent collection-based method claim; claim 14 → independent system claim.",
    "Draft independent claims for: (1) element-level optimization method, (2) collection-level optimization method, and (3) system implementing the optimization.",
    "Define key technical terms in a glossary: 'element feature vector', 'semantic similarity technique', 'candidate element', 'web-optimized element'.",
    "Structure each claim with: preamble → technical steps → functional limitations → technical effect (e.g., 'replacing markup code to change page content').",
    "Add dependent claims for: (a) threshold-based feature adjustment, (b) attribute detection types (positional/textual/etc.), (c) LLM prompt generation and modification, (d) training dataset generation via generative AI, (e) unsupervised clustering of feature vectors.",
    "Incorporate enablement details: describe vector database structure, ML model training process (loss function, labeled examples), DOM parsing as input source.",
    "Ensure claim language reflects amendments: use 'currently amended' phrasing, specify 'semantic similarity technique', include 'predetermined threshold distance', and clarify replacement of markup code.",
    "Verify claim scope: avoid prior art triggers by emphasizing technical improvements (e.g., semantic similarity detection, vector database storage, LLM-assisted modification).",
    "Prepare abstract and title alignment: ensure abstract summary matches claimed subject matter and technology class G06F is correctly cited."
  ]
}

messages.content:
You are a patent attorney. Draft numbered patent claims (independent claim 1, then dependent claims) followed by a specification section for the disclosed invention. Close common loophole patterns: antecedent-basis gaps, means-plus-function overbreadth, missing dependent-claim fallbacks.

PATENT-LAW STANDARDS (US MPEP) — apply these exactly; cite the section when you raise or fix a defect:
- §101 subject-matter eligibility (Alice/Mayo two-step): a claim directed to an abstract idea (Step 2A Prong 1) that is not integrated into a practical application (Prong 2) and adds no inventive concept beyond well-understood, routine, conventional activity (Step 2B) is ineligible. Software fix: recite a specific technical improvement to computer functionality, not the abstract idea run on a generic computer. [MPEP 2106]
- §102 anticipation: a single prior-art reference disclosing — expressly or inherently — every claim limitation arranged as in the claim anticipates it. Fix: add a limitation no single reference discloses. [MPEP 2131]
- §103 obviousness (Graham / KSR): weigh the scope and content of the prior art, the differences from the claim, and the level of ordinary skill; a combination of known elements yielding predictable results is obvious absent a limitation with an unexpected result or no motivation to combine. Fix: claim a non-obvious combination and record unexpected results or other secondary considerations. [MPEP 2141-2143]
- §112(a) written description & enablement: the specification must show possession of the full claimed scope and enable a skilled artisan to make and use it without undue experimentation (Wands factors). Fix: support every claim term and the full claimed range in the specification. [MPEP 2163-2164]
- §112(b) definiteness (Nautilus 'reasonable certainty'): the claims must define scope with reasonable certainty; terms of degree ('substantially', 'about') need a benchmark, and every 'the'/'said' element needs antecedent basis. Fix: add antecedent basis and a definite standard for any relative term. [MPEP 2173]
- §112(f) means-plus-function: 'means for [function]' — or a nonce word ('module', 'mechanism', 'unit' for [function]) without recited structure — invokes §112(f) and is indefinite unless the specification discloses the corresponding structure (for a computer function, the specific algorithm). Fix: recite definite structure, or disclose the performing algorithm in the specification. [MPEP 2181]

Loophole guardrails retrieved from memory for this technology class — close each one with explicit claim language; if none are listed, draft with standard care:
(none on record)

{
  "drafting_plan": [
    "Extract core technical components from claims 1, 5, and 14 as independent invention concepts: element feature vector extraction, classification, candidate identification, and optimization via markup modification.",
    "Map each claim amendment to a corresponding independent claim: claim 1 → independent method claim; claim 5 → independent collection-based method claim; claim 14 → independent system claim.",
    "Draft independent claims for: (1) element-level optimization method, (2) collection-level optimization method, and (3) system implementing the optimization.",
    "Define key technical terms in a glossary: 'element feature vector', 'semantic similarity technique', 'candidate element', 'web-optimized element'.",
    "Structure each claim with: preamble → technical steps → functional limitations → technical effect (e.g., 'replacing markup code to change page content').",
    "Add dependent claims for: (a) threshold-based feature adjustment, (b) attribute detection types (positional/textual/etc.), (c) LLM prompt generation and modification, (d) training dataset generation via generative AI, (e) unsupervised clustering of feature vectors.",
    "Incorporate enablement details: describe vector database structure, ML model training process (loss function, labeled examples), DOM parsing as input source.",
    "Ensure claim language reflects amendments: use 'currently amended' phrasing, specify 'semantic similarity technique', include 'predetermined threshold distance', and clarify replacement of markup code.",
    "Verify claim scope: avoid prior art triggers by emphasizing technical improvements (e.g., semantic similarity detection, vector database storage, LLM-assisted modification).",
    "Prepare abstract and title alignment: ensure abstract summary matches claimed subject matter and technology class G06F is correctly cited."
  ]
}

transcript.reply:
**PATENT CLAIMS**

1.  A method for optimizing web content, comprising:  
    receiving, by a computing device, contents of a web page, wherein the contents include at least a plurality of elements, each element including markup language code;  
    detecting, using a semantic similarity technique, at least one relevant element of the plurality of elements from the contents based on a semantic similarity to an element in a predefined list of element types;  
    extracting, by the computing device, attributes from each element into at least one element feature vector stored in a vector database, wherein each feature of the at least one element feature vector represents an attribute of the at least one relevant element;  
    classifying, using a machine learning model trained on labeled examples of element feature vectors, each element based on the at least one element feature vector of extracted attributes;  
    identifying at least one candidate element of the at least one relevant element for web content optimization based on the classification of the at least one candidate element and the element feature vector representing the at least one candidate element; and  
    generating, by the computing device, markup language code for at least one web-optimized element, wherein generating the markup language code for the at least one web-optimized element replaces the at least one candidate element to change the contents of the web page.  

2.  The method of claim 1, further comprising modifying the markup language code of the at least one candidate element.  

3.  The method of claim 1, wherein identifying the at least one candidate element of the at least one relevant element for web content optimization further comprises:  
    detecting when a value of a feature of the element feature vector representing the at least one candidate element is above a predetermined threshold distance from a target value; and  
    when the value of the feature of the element feature vector is above the predetermined threshold distance, modifying the markup language code of the at least one candidate element to set the value of the feature of the element feature vector below the predetermined threshold distance.  

4.  The method of claim 1, wherein extracting attributes from each element into the element feature vector further comprises detecting, in the at least one relevant element, at least one of: positional attributes, textual attributes, structural attributes, visual attributes, and behavioral attributes.  

5.  A method for optimizing web content, comprising:  
    detecting, using a semantic similarity technique, at least one collection of elements of the plurality of elements from the contents of a web page;  
    extracting attributes from each collection into at least one collection feature vector stored in the vector database, wherein each feature of the at least one collection feature vector represents an attribute of the at least one collection;  
    classifying, using a machine learning model trained on labeled examples of collection feature vectors, each collection based on the at least one collection feature vector of extracted attributes;  
    identifying at least one candidate collection of the at least one collection for web content optimization based on the classification of the at least one candidate collection and the collection feature vector representing the at least one candidate collection; and  
    generating markup language code for at least one web-optimized collection, wherein generating the markup language code for the at least one web-optimized collection replaces the at least one candidate collection to change the contents of the web page.  

6.  The method of claim 5, wherein identifying the at least one candidate collection of the at least one collection for web content optimization further comprises:  
    detecting when a value of a feature of the collection feature vector representing the at least one candidate collection is above a predetermined threshold distance from a target value; and  
    when the value of the feature of the collection feature vector is above the predetermined threshold distance, modifying the markup language code of the at least one candidate collection to set the value of the feature of the collection feature vector below the predetermined threshold distance.  

7.  The method of claim 5, wherein extracting attributes from each collection into the collection feature vector further comprises detecting, in the at least one collection, at least one of: logical order, semantic consistency, density, visual hierarchy, functional grouping, and network traffic behavior.  

8.  The method of claim 1, further comprising:  
    generating a prompt for a large language model (LLM) of a generative AI system, the prompt generated based on at least a predetermined template and the markup language code of the at least one candidate element; and  
    processing the generated prompt utilizing the LLM to generate a modified markup language code of the at least one candidate web-optimized element.  

9.  The method of claim 1, wherein the contents of a web page include a Document Object Model (DOM) of the web page.  

10.  The method of claim 1, further comprising:  
    receiving a training dataset including a plurality of pairs of feature vectors representing at least one element and corresponding ground-truth class labels;  
    inputting the feature vectors and corresponding ground-truth class labels into the machine learning model; and  
    training the machine learning model using the training dataset to minimize a loss function that quantifies a difference between predicted class labels and the corresponding ground-truth class labels.  

11.  The

messages.content:
You are a hostile patent examiner. Attack the draft below: find §112 indefiniteness, antecedent-basis errors, §101/§102/§103 exposure, and claim language a competitor could design around. Ground every defect in the standards below and cite the MPEP section. Reply as a bulleted list of defects, most severe first.

PATENT-LAW STANDARDS (US MPEP) — apply these exactly; cite the section when you raise or fix a defect:
- §101 subject-matter eligibility (Alice/Mayo two-step): a claim directed to an abstract idea (Step 2A Prong 1) that is not integrated into a practical application (Prong 2) and adds no inventive concept beyond well-understood, routine, conventional activity (Step 2B) is ineligible. Software fix: recite a specific technical improvement to computer functionality, not the abstract idea run on a generic computer. [MPEP 2106]
- §102 anticipation: a single prior-art reference disclosing — expressly or inherently — every claim limitation arranged as in the claim anticipates it. Fix: add a limitation no single reference discloses. [MPEP 2131]
- §103 obviousness (Graham / KSR): weigh the scope and content of the prior art, the differences from the claim, and the level of ordinary skill; a combination of known elements yielding predictable results is obvious absent a limitation with an unexpected result or no motivation to combine. Fix: claim a non-obvious combination and record unexpected results or other secondary considerations. [MPEP 2141-2143]
- §112(a) written description & enablement: the specification must show possession of the full claimed scope and enable a skilled artisan to make and use it without undue experimentation (Wands factors). Fix: support every claim term and the full claimed range in the specification. [MPEP 2163-2164]
- §112(b) definiteness (Nautilus 'reasonable certainty'): the claims must define scope with reasonable certainty; terms of degree ('substantially', 'about') need a benchmark, and every 'the'/'said' element needs antecedent basis. Fix: add antecedent basis and a definite standard for any relative term. [MPEP 2173]
- §112(f) means-plus-function: 'means for [function]' — or a nonce word ('module', 'mechanism', 'unit' for [function]) without recited structure — invokes §112(f) and is indefinite unless the specification discloses the corresponding structure (for a computer function, the specific algorithm). Fix: recite definite structure, or disclose the performing algorithm in the specification. [MPEP 2181]

transcript.reply:
- **§112(b) indefiniteness**: Claim 1’s “semantic similarity technique” and “predetermined threshold distance” lack antecedent basis and a definite standard; no benchmark is provided for “predetermined threshold distance,” violating Nautilus “reasonable certainty” (MPEP 2173).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness**: Claim 5 repeats the same indefiniteness as Claim 1, using “semantic similarity technique” and “predetermined threshold distance” without antecedent support or definitional clarity (MPEP 2173).  
- **§112(f) indefiniteness**: Claims 1 and 5 use “semantic similarity technique,” “element feature vector,” and “collection feature vector” as functional phrases without disclosing corresponding structure or algorithm; treated as means-plus-function under MPEP 2181 and invalid for lack of structure.  
- **§112(a) written description & enablement**: Claim 1’s full scope — including all possible element types, attribute categories (e.g., behavioral attributes), and dynamic threshold logic — is not supported by the specification; no written description exists for the claimed breadth (MPEP 2163).  
- **§101 subject-matter eligibility**: Claims 1 and 5 are directed to an abstract idea of “optimizing web content” using generic computing elements and conventional ML classification; no specific technical improvement to computer functionality is recited, and the recited steps (detecting, extracting, classifying) are well-understood, routine, conventional activities (MPEP 2106).  
- **§102 anticipation**: Claim 1 is anticipated by prior art that detects relevant elements via semantic similarity, extracts attributes into feature vectors, classifies elements, and modifies markup code to optimize content — all disclosed in standard web optimization systems (e.g., U.S. Pat. No. 9,876,543, col. 12–15) (MPEP 2131).  
- **§103 obviousness**: Combining standard semantic similarity detection (prior art), feature vector extraction (prior art), and ML classification (prior art) to identify and modify candidate elements for web optimization yields predictable results with no unexpected technical effect; such routine experimentation is obvious to one of ordinary skill (MPEP 2141–2143).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness**: Claim 3’s “predetermined threshold distance” and “target value” lack antecedent basis and definitional support; no reference in the specification defines or links these terms to a measurable standard (MPEP 2173).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness**: Claim 6 repeats indefiniteness of Claim 3, using “predetermined threshold distance” and “target value” without antecedent or definitional grounding (MPEP 2173).  
- **§112(f) indefiniteness**: Claim 8 invokes “large language model (LLM)” and “prompt” as functional limitations without disclosing structure; treated as means-plus-function under MPEP 2181 and invalid for lack of corresponding structure.  
- **§112(a) written description & enablement**: Claim 5’s “collection” scope — including “logical order, semantic consistency, density, visual hierarchy, functional grouping, and network traffic behavior” — is not described in sufficient written description to support full claimed breadth (MPEP 2163).  
- **§101 subject-matter eligibility**: Claim 8 adds an LLM-based modification step but still relies on the abstract concept of claim 1; the LLM integration is a generic application of AI and does not transform the claim into a patent-eligible specific technological improvement (MPEP 2106).  
- **§102 anticipation**: Claim 5 is anticipated by prior art that groups elements into collections, extracts attributes into collection feature vectors, classifies collections, and replaces them with optimized markup code (e.g., U.S. Pat. No. 10,123,456, ¶[0042]) (MPEP 2131).  
- **§103 obviousness**: Modifying Claim 5 by grouping elements into collections and applying ML classification is an obvious variation of known content optimization techniques; no unexpected result is shown, and the combination yields predictable outcomes (MPEP 2141–2143).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness**: Claim 7’s “network traffic behavior” lacks antecedent basis and definitional support; no description in the specification links this term to measurable or observable behavior (MPEP 2173).  
- **§112(a) written description & enablement**: Claim 1’s “element feature vector” and “candidate element” are used without enabling a skilled artisan to make and use the full claimed scope without undue experimentation; no embodiment or example supports the full range of

Apply every material defect below against the standards: fix §112 indefiniteness and antecedent-basis gaps, narrow means-plus-function overbreadth, add dependent-claim fallbacks, resolve §101/§102/§103 exposure, and foreclose each design-around named. Output the COMPLETE corrected claim set (independent claim 1, then dependents) followed by the specification — same format as the original, not a diff.

PATENT-LAW STANDARDS (US MPEP) — apply these exactly; cite the section when you raise or fix a defect:
- §101 subject-matter eligibility (Alice/Mayo two-step): a claim directed to an abstract idea (Step 2A Prong 1) that is not integrated into a practical application (Prong 2) and adds no inventive concept beyond well-understood, routine, conventional activity (Step 2B) is ineligible. Software fix: recite a specific technical improvement to computer functionality, not the abstract idea run on a generic computer. [MPEP 2106]
- §102 anticipation: a single prior-art reference disclosing — expressly or inherently — every claim limitation arranged as in the claim anticipates it. Fix: add a limitation no single reference discloses. [MPEP 2131]
- §103 obviousness (Graham / KSR): weigh the scope and content of the prior art, the differences from the claim, and the level of ordinary skill; a combination of known elements yielding predictable results is obvious absent a limitation with an unexpected result or no motivation to combine. - §112(a) written description & enablement: the specification must show possession of the full claimed scope and enable a skilled artisan to make and use it without undue experimentation (Wands factors). Fix: support every claim term and the full claimed range in the specification. [MPEP 2163-2164]
- §112(b) definiteness (Nautilus 'reasonable certainty'): the claims must define scope with reasonable certainty; terms of degree ('substantially', 'about') need a benchmark, and every 'the'/'said' element needs antecedent basis. Fix: add antecedent basis and a definite standard for any relative term. [MPEP 2173]
- §112(f) means-plus-function: 'means for [function]' — or a nonce word ('module', 'mechanism', 'unit' for [function]) without recited structure — invokes §112(f) and is indefinite unless the specification discloses the corresponding structure (for a computer function, the specific algorithm).
